title: Late Afternoon
commentary: Markets consolidate near session highs as the Iran relief rally matures—S&P holds 6,889 (+0.2%), Nasdaq +0.5%, with buyers defending every pullback attempt. Crypto cools slightly but maintains gains: Bitcoin steady at $69,000 (+4% on day), Ethereum $2,030 (+2.5%), Solana +3.9%—the "buy the geopolitical dip" trade fully played out. Oil continues to retreat, now at $73, down from morning's $79 panic high as Strait of Hormuz fears ease. VIX crushed back to 18.5 territory; market seemingly pricing in Iran tensions as contained. Two hours to the close—bulls looking to defend gains into the bell.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: Equities grind higher into early afternoon—S&P touches session high at 6,888 (+0.1% vs Friday close), completing a remarkable V-shaped recovery from the Iran-driven selloff at the open. Crypto consolidates gains with Bitcoin holding $69,000 (+4.5% on day), Ethereum steady near $2,040 (+3.3%); CMC20 index up 4.3% as the sector outperforms tradfi. Oil continues to retreat from morning panic highs, settling around $74—market pricing in containment of Middle East tensions. Defensive rotation unwinds as VIX drops below 19; traders now looking ahead to Tuesday's ISM services data. The "buy every dip" playbook continues to work.

title: Afternoon Rally
commentary: Risk-on in full swing as geopolitical fears fade into the rearview—S&P pushes into green territory at 6,895 (+0.3%), Nasdaq leads with +0.6% as megacap tech catches a bid. Crypto steals the show with spot BTC ETFs attracting $787M in inflows—Bitcoin punches through $69,600 (+5.1% on day), Ethereum clears $2,060 (+4.5%), Solana rips nearly 6%. Oil retreats further to $75 as Strait of Hormuz panic unwinds; gold consolidates gains near $5,360. The Iran dip now looking like a classic buy-the-news opportunity—bulls firmly in control of the tape.

title: Midday Reversal
commentary: The tape flips green as dip buyers overwhelm Iran fears—S&P erases morning losses to trade flat at 6,870, Nasdaq turns positive as tech leads the reversal. Crypto explodes higher with Bitcoin surging past $69,000 (+3% hourly), Ethereum reclaiming $2,040 (+3.5%), and Solana ripping 4%+ as risk appetite returns in force. Defense stocks consolidate gains while airlines attempt to bounce from morning lows. Oil retreats to $77 from session highs as Strait of Hormuz concerns ease marginally; gold holds elevated at $5,380. The "buy the dip" crowd making their presence felt—VIX dropping from morning spike.

title: Morning Recovery
commentary: Wall Street finds its footing after Iran-shock open—S&P pares losses to -0.5% at 6,846, Dow -0.6%, Nasdaq -0.5% as dip buyers emerge in late-morning trade. Crypto stages a sharp rebound with Bitcoin reclaiming $67,000 (+2.6% hourly) and Ethereum pushing back toward $1,980 (+2.9% hourly) after overnight carnage. Oil holds elevated near $79 but retreats from earlier highs as initial panic subsides; gold stays bid on safe-haven flows. Treasury yields defy the risk-off playbook, with 10Y climbing back above 4%—traders unconvinced Iran conflict warrants full haven rotation. Asia closed deep red (Hang Seng -2.1%, Nikkei -1.4%) but U.S. trying to shake off geopolitical overhang.

title: Iran Crisis Rocks Markets
commentary: Geopolitical shock dominates Monday's tape as U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran send oil surging toward $80 and Strait of Hormuz shipping fears escalate—Dow futures plunge 500 points. European stocks crater 2%+ with Stoxx 600 in full risk-off mode; Asian markets already battered with Hang Seng -2.1%, Nikkei -1.4%. Crypto takes a hit—Bitcoin drops to $65,390 (-2.4%), Ethereum slides to $1,924 (-4.4%) as risk assets flee. Gold jumps on safe-haven flows while Treasury yields defy the playbook, rising despite chaos. Defense stocks soar; airlines sink. This is the March volatility traders feared.

title: After Hours
commentary: Markets closed for the weekend after a punishing February finale. Crypto extends losses in thin after-hours trade—Bitcoin slips to $64,500 (-4.2% on day), testing January support as weekend risk-off takes hold; Ethereum drops to $1,870 (-6.1%), breaking below key $1,900 level. Futures flat with no catalyst until Monday's ISM manufacturing data. Gold holds at $5,260 in overseas trade; oil steady at $64.80. Traders lick wounds after hot PCE data crushed rate-cut hopes—March volatility looking increasingly likely.

title: Weekly Close
commentary: Wall Street wraps a brutal week in the red: S&P closes -1.4% at 48,890 for a weekly loss of 2.8%, Nasdaq -1.7% as tech capitulates on hot PCE and stagflation fears. Crypto carnage continues—Bitcoin settles at $64,800 (-3.8%), its worst week since January; Ethereum craters to $1,890 (-5.1%) with DeFi liquidations mounting. Gold catches a bid to close at $5,260 (+0.4%) as safe-haven flows accelerate; oil tanks to $64.80 (-2.8%) on growth concerns. February ends on a sour note—traders brace for volatility as March looms.

title: Afternoon Session
commentary: Selling pressure mounts into the final hours: S&P extends losses to -1.2% at 48,970, Nasdaq -1.5% as growth stocks crack—tech breadth deteriorating. Crypto accelerates lower—Bitcoin breaks through support to $65,300 (-3.0%), Ethereum slides to $1,918 (-4.3%) with leveraged positions unwinding. Gold slips to $5,245 (+0.1%) as dollar surges on hawkish rate repricing; oil drops to $65.10 (-2.3%). VIX climbs to 21.2—month-end selloff gaining momentum as funds lock in losses.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: Markets churn sideways as traders digest hot PCE: S&P holds at -0.9% near 49,120, Nasdaq -1.2% with tech finding tentative support. Fed rate cut expectations for May now down to 15% from 40% pre-data—higher for longer narrative regains traction. Crypto treads water—Bitcoin steady at $65,543 (-1.9%), Ethereum consolidates at $1,925 (-3.2%). Gold eases to $5,260 (+0.3%) as dollar catches a bid on yield differentials; oil flat at $65.50 (-1.7%). Month-end rebalancing flows could add volatility into the close.
